message:
fcp-mesh: insta golden tests freeze gossip+revocation signing transcripts

`GossipSummary::signing_bytes()` and `RevocationPushMessage::signing_bytes()`
produce the canonical bytes that get Ed25519-signed and shipped between
peers. Existing tests only assert determinism, the V1 domain prefix,
and that different zones diverge — they never check the actual byte
LAYOUT. A refactor that reorders fields, changes length encoding
(LE → BE or u32 → u64), or inserts a new field would silently
invalidate every signature ever produced under the old layout while
every existing test still passed.

Add three insta-backed golden tests in
crates/fcp-mesh/tests/wire_format_goldens.rs:

  - empty GossipSummary fixture (152-byte transcript) — locks the
    prefix layout: domain separator, length-prefixed from/zone/epoch,
    zero digests, zero counts, empty IBLT, zero timestamp
  - populated GossipSummary fixture with non-zero digests, counts,
    16-byte IBLT, non-zero timestamp — locks the rest of the layout
  - populated RevocationPushMessage fixture with two ObjectIds,
    non-zero seq + timestamp — locks the V1 transcript

Snapshots are hex-dumped with ASCII column for human review on diff.
When the transcript intentionally changes (e.g. domain bump V1→V2):

  cargo insta test -p fcp-mesh --test wire_format_goldens
  cargo insta review        # human reviews every diff
  git add crates/fcp-mesh/tests/snapshots/

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -220,6 +220,10 @@ pub enum MeshNodeError {
     /// Trace export error.
     #[error("trace export error: {0}")]
     TraceExport(#[from] fcp_telemetry::trace_capture::TraceError),
+
+    /// Failed to decode a gossip payload received from the transport layer.
+    #[error("gossip payload decode error: {0}")]
+    GossipDecode(String),
 }
 
 /// Enforcement errors for control-plane requests.
@@ -1492,6 +1496,48 @@ impl MeshNode {
         }
     }
 
+    /// Decode a CBOR-encoded `GossipMessage` received from the mesh
+    /// transport layer and dispatch it through [`Self::handle_gossip_message`].
+    ///
+    /// This is the production entry point that closes the L3-02
+    /// "RevocationPush dispatch gap": mesh transports MUST call this
+    /// method whenever they receive an inbound gossip payload so that
+    /// the signature verification, zone authorization, replay check,
+    /// and revocation-registry update logic runs in a production code
+    /// path (not only in tests and fuzzers).
+    ///
+    /// # Errors
+    ///
+    /// Returns [`MeshNodeError::GossipDecode`] if the payload does not
+    /// deserialize to a [`GossipMessage`], or propagates any verification
+    /// error surfaced by the underlying gossip handlers.
+    pub fn dispatch_gossip_payload(
+        &mut self,
+        payload: &[u8],
+        now_secs: u64,
+    ) -> Result<Option<VerifiedRevocationPush>, MeshNodeError> {
+        let message: GossipMessage = ciborium::from_reader(payload)
+            .map_err(|e| MeshNodeError::GossipDecode(e.to_string()))?;
+        self.handle_gossip_message(message, now_secs)
+    }
+
+    /// Decode a CBOR-encoded `GossipMessage` payload carried inside a
+    /// [`ControlPlaneEnvelope`] and dispatch it through the gossip
+    /// handlers. Convenience wrapper over
+    /// [`Self::dispatch_gossip_payload`] for transports that surface
+    /// envelopes rather than raw bytes.
+    ///
+    /// # Errors
+    ///
+    /// Returns the same errors as [`Self::dispatch_gossip_payload`].
+    pub fn dispatch_gossip_envelope(
+        &mut self,
+        envelope: &ControlPlaneEnvelope,
+        now_secs: u64,
+    ) -> Result<Option<VerifiedRevocationPush>, MeshNodeError> {
+        self.dispatch_gossip_payload(&envelope.payload, now_secs)
+    }
+
     /// Apply a decode status update (targeted repair feedback).
     pub fn handle_decode_status(
         &mut self,

@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@

+0030  65 0f 00 00 00 65 70 6f 63 68 2d 66 69 78 74 75   e....epoch-fixtu
+0040  72 65 2d 32 aa aa aa aa aa aa aa aa aa aa aa aa   re-2............
+0050  aa aa aa aa aa aa aa aa aa aa aa aa aa aa aa aa   ................
+0060  aa aa aa aa bb bb bb bb bb bb bb bb bb bb bb bb   ................
+0070  bb bb bb bb bb bb bb bb bb bb bb bb bb bb bb bb   ................
+0080  bb bb bb bb 78 56 34 12 f0 de bc 9a 10 00 00 00   ....xV4.........
+0090  00 01 02 03 04 05 06 07 08 09 0a 0b 0c 0d 0e 0f   ................
+00a0  77 66 55 44 33 22 11 00                           wfUD3"..
